reasoning: The Lean statement correctly captures the informal Putnam 1998 B6 claim. The universal over a, b, c ∈ ℤ matches, and `∃ n : ℤ, n > 0` faithfully encodes "there exists a positive integer n". The condition `¬ ∃ m : ℤ, Real.sqrt(P(n)) = m` correctly expresses that √P(n) is not an integer: under Mathlib's convention (sqrt of non-positive reals returns 0), this holds precisely when P(n) > 0 and P(n) is not a perfect square — which is the standard mathematical reading of "√P(n) is not an integer" (requiring √P(n) to exist as a real and not equal any integer; note √0 = 0 is an integer, matching the informal reading). The integer polynomial is correctly coerced to ℝ for Real.sqrt. This is the natural and faithful formalization.
